human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for securities-settlement boundary discipline, source quality, allocation and affirmation workflow posture, SSI and depository evidence handling, exception and fail routing, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to securities-settlement execution such as allocation and affirmation dependency review, SSI validation, depository progression, fail handling, cash and position blocker routing, and completion evidence capture.
Authority sources cover SEC settlement-cycle rulemaking and exam guidance, DTCC matching and depository workflow references, and FINRA or SEC books-and-records control standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete artifacts, rework queues, completion-evidence refusal, realistic settlement terminology, conflicting-policy handling, overgeneralization guardrails, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/securities-settlements-specialist/ so settlement work stays distinct from trade support, treasury, corporate actions, securities financing, legal, and compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around funding approvals, legal interpretation, sanctions or AML disposition, asset-class overgeneralization, and completion without evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated securities-settlements specialist that supports evidence-backed post-trade settlement progression, exception routing, and audit-ready closure outputs.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
